config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, kepentingan masyarakat memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu karena hak individu justru menjadi batas agar mayoritas tidak semena-mena terhadap minoritas. Dalam praktiknya, kalau hak pribadi terlalu mudah dikorbankan atas nama kepentingan umum, kekuasaan bisa disalahgunakan dan ketidakadilan jadi dianggap wajar. Bagi saya, yang benar adalah mencari keseimbangan, bukan menempatkan masyarakat selalu di atas individu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From an American perspective, that idea sets off alarm bells because our history is full of examples where “the public good” was used to justify segregation, censorship, surveillance, and discrimination. Society does have legitimate interests, especially in public safety and preventing harm, but those interests need hard limits or they can easily become a license for the majority to trample minorities. The right balance is not “society first” but “individual rights first, except in narrowly defined cases where real harm to others is at stake.”

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan penggusuran, pembatasan kebebasan berpendapat, atau perlakuan tidak adil terhadap kelompok kecil, jadi saya tidak percaya mayoritas selalu lebih bijak. Memang ada kepentingan bersama seperti keamanan, kesehatan publik, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu harus dibatasi ketat supaya tidak berubah menjadi alat menekan orang yang lemah. Menurut saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru menjaga hak individu sebagai pagar, bukan mengorbankannya setiap kali ada klaim demi kebaikan bersama.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From a United States perspective, that kind of blanket claim is dangerous because it has repeatedly been used to excuse abuses like segregation, censorship, surveillance, and discrimination in the name of the common good. Society absolutely has real interests, but I do not trust majorities or governments to define those interests fairly without strong limits, especially when vulnerable minorities are involved. To me, the better principle is that individual rights come first, with only narrow exceptions where there is clear, direct harm to others.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman saya melihat Indonesia, dalih “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan tindakan yang merugikan warga kecil, jadi saya tidak percaya mayoritas otomatis lebih adil atau lebih bijak. Kepentingan bersama memang ada, terutama soal keamanan, kesehatan publik, dan ketertiban, tetapi itu harus dibatasi jelas agar tidak berubah menjadi alat penindasan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru berdiri di atas perlindungan hak individu, bukan pengorbanan hak itu setiap kali ada alasan kolektif.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society should have blanket priority over the rights of the individual, because in the United States that idea has too often been used to justify abuse in the name of the common good. The strongest point the other side can make is that public safety, health, and order are real collective interests, and I do not deny that; but those are exactly the areas where governments can overreach if we give them too much trust. In my view, individual rights should be the default priority, with society allowed to limit them only when there is a clear, concrete harm to others and the restriction is tightly limited. If we reverse that order, minorities and unpopular people are usually the first to pay the price.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
